title: 9 Herbal Creams For Cystitis
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great relief for cystitis, a painful and uncomfortable urinary tract infection. But why do herbal teas also help to ease the symptoms? The answer lies in the way these herbs interact with the body. Urtica dioica, or stinging nettle, has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the bladder and urinary tract. Calendula officinalis, or marigold, has antimicrobial properties that help fight off the infection and promote healing. Aloe barbadensis, or aloe vera, has soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that calm the bladder and reduce discomfort.  When you drink herbal teas made from these herbs, the active compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and travel to the affected area. This can help to reduce inflammation, kill off bacteria, and promote healing. As a result, you may experience relief from painful urination, frequent trips to the bathroom, and other uncomfortable symptoms of cystitis. The benefits of using herbal teas to relieve cystitis are numerous. They can be a natural and non-invasive way to manage symptoms, avoiding the need for antibiotics or other medications. They can also be used in conjunction with conventional treatments, such as antibiotics, to help promote healing and reduce the risk of complications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica creams contains bioactive constituents like histamine, polysaccharides, and mucilages. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and alleviate the symptoms of cystitis, such as burning and frequent urination. The mucilages in Urtica dioica creams create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, soothing and calming the irritated tissues. The anti-inflammatory properties of histamine and polysaccharides also help to reduce swelling and pain in the bladder and urethra. By reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes, Urtica dioica creams can help to provide relief from the symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and a double boiler.
Wash the Urtica dioica leaves with water and chop them. In the double boiler, melt the coconut oil.
Add the chopped Urtica dioica leaves to the melted coconut oil. Let it simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl. Discard the solids.
Transfer the cooled mixture to a jar and store it in the fridge. Use 1-2 teaspoons of the cream as needed for cystitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains flavonoids, triterpenoids, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the urinary tract. The flavonoids in calendula cream, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The triterpenoids in calendula cream, including ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, have been found to have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections that can cause cystitis. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacterial infections, calendula cream can provide relief from the symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers and 1/2 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil).
Combine the dried flowers and carrier oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.

Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the bowl. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating, until the beeswax and shea butter are melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean container. Let it cool and harden before use. Store the cream in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-verboside, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that cause cystitis. Aloe-emodin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that can contribute to cystitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe barbadensis creams also help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing discomfort and pain associated with cystitis. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacterial infections, aloe barbadensis creams can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of tea tree essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in the shea butter and Aloe barbadensis gel until well combined.
Add the vitamin E oil and tea tree essential oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean container.
Let the mixture cool and solidify. Once set, scoop out the cream as needed to apply to the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains alkylamides, glycoproteins, and polyphenols as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with cystitis. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams also have immune-boosting properties, which can help the body fight off bacterial infections that cause cystitis. The glycoproteins in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce bacterial growth and inflammation in the urinary tract. By reducing inflammation and fighting off bacterial infections, Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate symptoms of cystitis and promote healing in the urinary tract.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of tea tree oil.
Dry the Echinacea flowers completely in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments.
Add the dried Echinacea flowers, shea butter, and tea tree oil to the melted mixture. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, and naphthodianthrones, including hyperforin and emodin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the urinary tract. The flavonoids also have a soothing effect on the bladder and urethra, which can ease burning sensations and discomfort associated with cystitis. Additionally, hyperforin has a natural antiseptic action that helps combat bacterial infections that can cause cystitis. By applying Hypericum perforatum creams directly to the affected area, it may provide relief from the symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 100g dried Hypericum perforatum flowers, 100g coconut oil, 100g beeswax, 2 tablespoons shea butter, and 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add dried Hypericum perforatum flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix in shea butter and vitamin E oil until well combined. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains active constituents like rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and apigenin. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with cystitis. The antimicrobial properties of Melissa officinalis inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria, such as E. coli, that cause cystitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of rosmarinic acid and luteolin also help alleviate pain and discomfort in the urinary tract. By reducing inflammation and combating bacterial infections, Melissa officinalis creams can provide relief from the symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Infuse the coconut oil with Melissa officinalis flowers: Combine the flowers and oil in a saucepan and heat it over low heat for 2 hours.
Strain the oil: Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the flowers.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter: Combine the strained oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a saucepan and heat it over low heat until the beeswax melts.
Add vitamin E oil and pour into a container: Add the vitamin E oil and pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense creams contains equifolin, equisetic acid, and caffeic acid as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with cystitis. The flavonoids in Equisetum arvense creams also have a protective effect on the bladder and urinary tract, reducing inflammation and irritation. The antioxidant properties of caffeic acid and equisetic acid help neutralize free radicals and promote healing in the affected area. The combination of these properties makes Equisetum arvense creams a potential treatment option for cystitis, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote a speedy recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Equisetum arvense leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the Equisetum arvense leaves. Let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the leaves. Let the liquid cool down to room temperature.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the cooled liquid with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax melts. Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis creams contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that help soothe inflamed tissues. The major mucilage constituent of Althaea officinalis is polysaccharide, specifically galactose, rhamnose, and glucuronic acid. These mucilages have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and redness, which makes them effective in treating the painful inflammation associated with cystitis. Additionally, the galactomannans present in Althaea officinalis have antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacterial infections that can cause cystitis. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Althaea officinalis creams can provide relief from the symptoms of cystitis.
remedies.image_alt: Althaea officinalis herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Althaea officinalis root, 2 cups of distilled water, and a clean pot.
Combine the dried root and water in the pot, heat on low until the mixture boils.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 2 hours, then strain the mixture into a clean bowl.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the bowl, stir until melted and mixed.
Pour the mixture into small containers, let it cool and harden. Use the Althaea officinalis cream as needed for cystitis relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus creams contains bioactive constituents like anthocyanins and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm the urinary tract. The anthocyanins, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause cystitis, such as E. coli. The flavonoids also help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections, including pain and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting bacterial growth, Vaccinium myrtillus creams may provide relief from cystitis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Vaccinium myrtillus herbal creams for cystitis
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Vaccinium myrtillus berries, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Mash the Vaccinium myrtillus berries in a bowl to release their juice.
Combine the mashed berries, coconut oil, and beeswax in a saucepan and heat it on low heat, stirring until the beeswax melts.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the Vaccinium myrtillus cream in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent cystitis. Certain herbs like marshmallow root and juniper berries have anti-inflammatory properties that could soothe the urinary tract and reduce irritation. Some studies also suggest that herbal teas like sage and peppermint may help reduce bacterial growth and alleviate symptoms.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can help with cystitis symptoms, drinking them daily may not be the best solution. Some teas, like juniper and uva ursi, can irritate the bladder and make the problem worse. It's essential to balance tea consumption with a healthy lifestyle, including a balanced diet and plenty of water.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for cystitis can start working within a few hours. Some people find relief from discomfort and pain after a few sips. As the body absorbs the active ingredients, symptoms like burning urination and frequent trips to the bathroom may begin to decrease. Results can vary, but many people notice improvement within 24 to 48 hours.
faq_time: For cystitis, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This is when your body is most active, and the tea can help fight off the infection. The caffeine in tea can also help stimulate your bladder to start working properly, which is especially helpful when you're not feeling well.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas, like uva ursi and juniper berries, contain compounds that might affect how your body processes certain medications for cystitis. This could lead to changes in how your medication works or increase the risk of side effects. Be cautious when combining herbal teas with prescription medications, as it may impact their effectiveness or your overall health.
faq_pregnant: When it comes to cystitis during pregnancy, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like juniper and parsley, can stimulate the uterus and cause complications. If you still want to try herbal teas, look for ones that are specifically labeled as safe for pregnant women, and drink them in moderation.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding and experiencing cystitis, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like juniper and horsetail, can affect the baby's kidneys. However, others like peppermint, chamomile, and calendula are generally considered safe. They may even help soothe the bladder and reduce discomfort.
title_2: 9 Natural Herbal Creams for Women Suffering from Cystitis
